verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is a soluble F1 catalytic subunit of ATP synthase, residing on the mitochondrial inner membrane matrix-side at baseline. However, extensive evidence documents translocation to the plasma membrane across multiple cell types (hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes) and cancer cells. This translocation occurs via microtubule-mediated trafficking (DRP1/KIF5B-dependent) and mitochondrial membrane fusion with the PM. This constitutes dual localization with a stable, documented PM pool, qualifying as contextual surface accessibility.
